title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for ecdh, x25519, and authenticated key agreement and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.
Select X25519 or approved NIST curves from protocol, hardware, compliance, interoperability, and implementation constraints.
Feed the ECDH result and full context into a labeled KDF rather than using raw shared bytes directly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 7568 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.

Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 8698 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral, keep Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions.
